title: D2 field-constraints spec
description: Schema for contracts/validation/field-constraints.spec.json — the source-of-truth catalog that DcsvIo.D2.Validation.SourceGen reads to emit FieldConstraints.g.cs + Taxonomy.g.cs into DcsvIo.D2.Validation.Abstractions, and that the TypeScript codegen pipeline reads to emit field-constraints.g.ts + taxonomy.g.ts into @dcsv-io/d2-validation-abstractions. One spec carries both the shared field-constraint integer bounds (char-length caps and phone digit-count bounds) and the closed-list taxonomy enums consumed by the domain value objects (contacts + Location), the FE/BFF Zod schemas, and arbitrary backend modules.

name.description: SCREAMING_SNAKE constant name. Must be unique across constraints. Becomes the emitted public const int name on both .NET and TS.

value.description: The positive integer bound. Must be greater than zero.

doc.description: XML <summary> text rendered on the emitted .NET constant + JSDoc on the emitted TS constant.

deprecated.description: When true, the entry is deprecated: the generated C# constant + factory carry [Obsolete] and the TS constant + factory carry @deprecated. Additive — the entry is NEVER deleted. Deleting a deprecated (or non-deprecated) entry is a breaking change the JSON-diff gate fails.

deprecatedReason.description: Plain dev-facing English explaining why the code is deprecated; rendered into the [Obsolete("...")] message, the @deprecated JSDoc, and the changelog entry. NOT a TK key (developer-facing, not user-facing).

replacedBy.description: Wire code of the successor entry (e.g. RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND). Must reference a real, non-deprecated entry in the merged registry. Omit for pure retirement with no replacement.

sunset.description: ISO-8601 date for the future RFC 8594 Sunset response header. Edge-runtime-deferred: carried on the contract now, consumed by the Edge response middleware when it exists. Inert until then.

name.description: PascalCase enum type name. Must be unique across enums. Becomes the emitted enum type name (.NET) / const-object + branded type name (TS).

backing.description: Underlying numeric backing type of the .NET enum. Only byte is supported (closed lists are small).

doc.description: XML <summary> text rendered on the emitted .NET enum + JSDoc on the emitted TS const-object.

deprecated.description: When true, the entry is deprecated: the generated C# constant + factory carry [Obsolete] and the TS constant + factory carry @deprecated. Additive — the entry is NEVER deleted. Deleting a deprecated (or non-deprecated) entry is a breaking change the JSON-diff gate fails.

deprecatedReason.description: Plain dev-facing English explaining why the code is deprecated; rendered into the [Obsolete("...")] message, the @deprecated JSDoc, and the changelog entry. NOT a TK key (developer-facing, not user-facing).

replacedBy.description: Wire code of the successor entry (e.g. RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND). Must reference a real, non-deprecated entry in the merged registry. Omit for pure retirement with no replacement.

sunset.description: ISO-8601 date for the future RFC 8594 Sunset response header. Edge-runtime-deferred: carried on the contract now, consumed by the Edge response middleware when it exists. Inert until then.

name.description: C# / TS identifier-safe member name. Must be unique within the enum. The wire form is the member name itself (string-wire via JsonStringEnumConverter on .NET; string-valued const-object on TS).

doc.description: XML <summary> text rendered on the emitted .NET enum member + JSDoc on the emitted TS member.
